top_signals.title: ECB’s Dolenc Says Hike Was Needed as Data Sound Inflation Alarm
top_signals.summary: European Central Bank Governing Council member Primoz Dolenc said Thursday’s interest-rate increase was necessary to keep prices in check while officials consider the broader implications of conflict in the Middle East.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: FTSE 100 Live: UK economy tipped to have shrank as Iran war impacts take hold
top_signals.summary: Good morning and welcome back to the City AM liveblog. We’re expecting fresh data at 7am this morning that will reveal the UK’s headline growth last month. A poll of City economists by Bloomberg suggests the economy will have contracted 0.1 per cent in April, which follows on from a 0.3 per cent expansion in [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: ‘You sell what you can, not what you want’: Jack Mallers says bitcoin is pricing a global liquidity crisis
top_signals.summary: Strike CEO Jack Mallers says Bitcoin at $63,000 reflects a liquidity-starved world, and questions Strategy's perpetual stock capital dynamic.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Potential liquidity or banking-system instability affecting financial conditions.

top_signals.title: Banking rails are moving past the 'stablecoin winner' narrative: Sygnum
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: China Asks Big Banks to Curb Interbank Lending to Ease Cash Glut
top_signals.summary: China told big state-owned banks to reduce their lending in the interbank market, according to people familiar with the matter, in an effort to prevent borrowing costs from drifting too far below the policy interest rate.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Rotterdam's Growing Oil Storage Fraud Problem Is Costing Traders Millions
top_signals.summary: The port of Rotterdam has seen, in recent years, increasingly sophisticated attempts by fraudsters to scam millions of dollars out of traders by paper fraud, offering non-existent oil storage at one of the world’s biggest oil hubs. The frauds, using fake documents and fraudulent websites, lure traders to pay for oil storage that simply doesn’t exist at the Port of Rotterdam. The scams have been running for about 15 years, the Port of Rotterdam Authority has estimated, and has created a special task force to fight them. Scammers are…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Do the Prem Rugby semi-finals need a Welsh URC team?
top_signals.summary: Ahead of this weekend’s Prem Rugby’s semi-finals, Ollie Phillips assesses whether they could do with a hint of Wales. Picture the scene: next year’s Prem Rugby semi-finals feature the top three teams from this year – Northampton Saints, Bath Rugby and Exeter Chiefs – plus one of the Scarlets, Ospreys, Dragons or Cardiff. This would, [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Cyber Force not included in Senate defense policy roadmap
top_signals.summary: An amendment by Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand (D-NY) to the chamber’s fiscal 2027 national defense authorization bill that would have created the digital-focused service was defeated 14-13 when the Senate Armed Services Committee took up the nearly $1.2 trillion legislation behind closed doors this week.
